response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, distributed systems, and developer tooling, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The job mandates 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum qualifier, yet the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — C++ does not even appear in his skills section. His primary languages are TypeScript, JavaScript, DLang, and Node.js. While he has meaningful exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby) and some cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), there is no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, formal technical leadership, or the infrastructure depth at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems, including micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby.
Broad infrastructure exposure including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and AWS CDK across multiple roles.
Community leadership through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects shows communication and mentoring capacity.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (roughly 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) versus the 8-year minimum requirement — this alone is a hard disqualifier.
No formal technical leadership role; staff-level titles appear without documented ownership of large-scale architecture initiatives or team technical direction.
No cloud compute platform experience; cloud exposure is limited to deployment tooling (Kubernetes, AWS CDK) rather than building or leading compute infrastructure.
